title: Infers concat in FIRRTL for output ports on LHS
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue
Having concatenations in the output ports of an instantiated module infers improper FIRRTL.
Run `yosys -s script`

script:
```
read_verilog dff.v
opt_clean
write_firrtl dff.fir
```
dff.v 
```
module bsg_dff
(
	input clk_i, 
	input [4-1:0] data_i,
	output [4-1:0] data_o
);

	reg [4-1:0] data_r;
	assign data_o = data_r;
	always @(posedge clk_i)
		data_r <= data_i;
endmodule

module bsg_dff_chain
(
	input clk_i,
	input [4-1:0] data_i,
	output [4-1:0] data_o
);

	reg [4-1:0] data_delayed;
	assign data_delayed = data_i;
	assign data_o = data_delayed[num_stages_p];

	bsg_dff
	ch_reg 
	(
		.clk_i(clk_i)
		,.data_i(data_delayed)
		,.data_o({data_delayed[3:1], data_delayed[3]})
	);
endmodule
```
## Expected behavior

1. Declare a wire that is the concatenation of output elements
2. Running `firrtl -i dff.fir -X verilog -o dff_converted.v` should generate roughly the same verilog.

## Actual behavior

1. Instead infers something like `Cat(a,b) <= c`
2. ~Doesn't generate verilog;~ throws this exception while running firrtl:
`Exception in thread "main" firrtl.passes.CheckHighFormLike$InvalidLOCException:  @[dff.v:26.2-31.3]: [module bsg_dff_chain_____] Invalid connect to an expression that is not a reference or a WritePort.`

## Yosys version

0.9+4306
